sections.paragraph:
εἶδος, ους, τό [cp. ἰδέα] ‘that which makes an optical impression’

—a. external form, aspect, appearance Lk 3:22 (of someth. that looked like a pigeon); 9:29 (of facial appearance); J 5:37 (has not seen what God looks like).

—b. appearance, of behavior 1 Th 5:22 (‘refrain from doing anything that looks bad’).

—c. sight, of circumstance 2 Cor 5:7 (‘we do not live on the basis of what we see’).
